stories.name: Beverly City Hall Renovations

events.summary: The City of Beverly is soliciting Statements of Qualifications from trade filed sub-bid contractors to prequalify for renovation of existing Beverly City Hall, demolition of the attached annex building down to its foundation, and construction of a new three-story addition. Filed sub-bid categories include Masonry, Roofing, Plumbing, Electrical, HVAC, and others. Interested parties must register and use Project Code 874345.

stories.name: Beverly High School Cafeteria Redesign and Renovation (Bid #26-015)

events.summary: The City of Beverly is seeking sealed bids for the redesign, renovation, and revitalization of the cafeteria space at Beverly High School (Bid #26-015). Sealed bids are due June 25, 2026 at 11:00 AM. Bid documents are available at https://www.beverlyma.gov/Bids.aspx or from the Office of the Purchasing Agent, Room 24, 191 Cabot St., Beverly City Hall during regular business hours.

stories.name: Wireless Telephone Communications Facility Folly Hill Water Tower
stories.address: Folly Hill Water Tower, Beverly, MA

events.summary: The City of Beverly is soliciting proposals for a wireless telephone communications facility at the Folly Hill Water Tower (Bid #26-014, CommBuys BD-26-1141-BEVLY-BEVLY-129611). This is a statewide open-market solicitation; no estimated contract value is stated. Bids close June 22, 2026 at 11:00 AM.

stories.name: ADU at 20 Roundy Street

events.summary: Applicant seeks a variance under §300-37 D(8) to demolish and reconstruct a pre-existing nonconforming accessory structure as an accessory dwelling unit, with a rear yard setback encroachment into the RHD district. Creates one new dwelling unit on an existing residential lot.

stories.name: Proposed Amendments to Beverly Zoning Ordinance — Accessory Dwelling Units

events.summary: The Beverly Planning Board will hold a public hearing on proposed amendments to the City Zoning Ordinance regarding accessory dwelling units (ADUs). No draft text, specific provisions, or scope of the amendment are described in the agenda.

stories.name: Glen Urquhart School Building Raze and Rebuild

brief.what: Glen Urquhart School proposes to raze an existing building at 206 Greenwood Avenue in Beverly, MA and replace it with a structure housing two classrooms, an exhibition hall, and administrative and meeting space, with a net reduction of over 1,000 sq ft of impervious surface and invasive species removal with native replanting.
brief.status: The Beverly Conservation Commission closed its public hearing on May 19, 2026 and issued an Order of Conditions approving the project, subject to invasive species management with two years of annual monitoring, dewatering controls during construction, and 75% planting survivorship as a condition of the final Certificate of Compliance.
brief.whats_next: The project must satisfy construction-phase conditions — dewatering with a sediment bag, invasive species management, and achieving 75% planting survivorship — before a Certificate of Compliance will be issued.
brief.outlook: With Conservation Commission approval secured and conditions clearly defined, the project appears positioned to move into permitting and construction without significant regulatory obstacles; the two-year post-construction monitoring requirement means the compliance timeline will extend well past project completion.

contacts.name: Glen Urquhart School c/o Lindsay Calef

events.summary: Beverly Conservation Commission opened and continued a Notice of Intent hearing for Glen Urquhart School's proposal to raze an existing building and construct a replacement at 206 Greenwood Avenue. The new building would house two classrooms, an exhibition hall, and administrative/meeting space, with a net reduction of over 1,000 sq ft of impervious surface across all buffer zones and the riverfront area; invasive species removal and native replanting are included as mitigation. A Commission site walk is scheduled for May 2, 2026, and the hearing is continued to May 19, 2026.

The Conservation Commission closed the public hearing and issued an Order of Conditions permitting Glen Urquhart School to raze and rebuild an existing building at 206 Greenwood Avenue. Conditions include invasive species management with two years of annual monitoring, dewatering with a sediment bag during construction, and 75% planting survivorship before a Certificate of Compliance will be issued.

stories.name: 23 Virginia Avenue ADU Staircase Addition

events.summary: The Commission issued a Negative 3 Determination clearing the proposal to create an accessory dwelling unit within the existing house at 23 Virginia Avenue. The project involves constructing an exterior enclosed staircase addition on helical piers at the rear of the house providing secondary egress to the second-floor ADU, sited more than 25 feet from the bordering vegetated wetland.

stories.name: Kennedy Accessory Dwelling Unit Addition — 7 Hathaway Avenue

events.summary: Beverly Conservation Commission opened and continued a Notice of Intent hearing for a 1,000 sq ft accessory dwelling unit addition with breezeway connection at 7 Hathaway Avenue, situated near a perennial stream and bordering vegetated wetland. The project also includes a two-car driveway (~650 sq ft), a 10x19 deck, and two drywells (1,000 and 500 gallons) to manage approximately 1,700 sq ft of new impervious surface; three replacement trees and an upland meadow habitat are proposed as mitigation. Hearing continued to May 19, 2026.

stories.name: 46 Federal Street Townhouses (SPR #170-26 / SP #205-26)

events.summary: The Beverly Planning Board unanimously approved Site Plan Review #170-26 and Special Permit #205-26 for 46 Federal Street LLC to demolish the existing dwelling and garage and construct five ownership-tenure townhouse units on an 8,000 sf lot with a 2,500 sf building footprint, approximately 37 feet tall. The special permit allows a 20-foot driveway aisle width in lieu of the required 24 feet; the ZBA had previously granted a dimensional variance on January 28, 2026. Approval is conditioned on requirements from the Parking & Traffic Commission, Board of Health, Design Review Board, and Engineering Department, and plans are drawn by Seger Architects.

stories.name: 26 Mason Street, Beverly, MA

brief.what: Albert Turner is seeking a variance at 26 Mason Street (RMD district) in Beverly, MA to construct a new detached single-car garage with an accessory dwelling unit on the second floor, under Beverly Zoning Ordinance §§300-36 D(6) and D(8).
brief.status: The variance hearing opened March 25, 2026 and was continued by the Zoning Board of Appeals at the April 22, 2026 session without a vote.
brief.whats_next: No next step stated in the record.
brief.outlook: A continuation after a single hearing is routine for variance applications and does not signal opposition, but the board has not yet acted; whether the project advances will depend on what outstanding questions prompted the continuation.

events.summary: New public hearing on a variance application to construct a new detached single-car garage with an accessory dwelling unit (ADU) on the second floor, pursuant to Sections 300-36 D(6) and D(8) of the Beverly Zoning Ordinance. Property is in the RMD zoning district; no further scope detail is provided in the agenda.

Albert Turner seeks a variance at 26 Mason Street (RMD district) to construct a new detached single-car garage with an accessory dwelling unit (ADU), per Sections 300-36 D(6) and D(8) of the Beverly Zoning Ordinance.

Continued variance hearing for construction of a new detached single-car garage with an Accessory Dwelling Unit above at 26 Mason Street in the RMD district, per Beverly Zoning Ordinance §§300-36 D(6) and D(8).

stories.name: 21 Bridge Street, Beverly, MA

brief.what: Jason Irving and 21 Bridge Street LLC are seeking a zoning variance to subdivide the existing lot at 21 Bridge Street in Beverly, MA's R10 district into two lots — retaining the current single-family home and creating a new lot for construction of an additional single-family home.
brief.status: The Zoning Board of Appeals hearing has been continued across at least three sessions, most recently on April 22, 2026, with no decision reached and no new continuation date stated in the record.
brief.whats_next: No next step or continuation date is stated in the record.
brief.outlook: Three consecutive continuations without a stated next date suggest the application is unresolved and may be facing procedural or substantive friction; forward momentum appears slow until the board schedules and closes the hearing.

events.summary: Continued hearing on a variance application to subdivide the existing lot, retaining the current single-family home and creating a new second lot for construction of an additional single-family home in the R10 district. The applicant represents the new lot and home are consistent with the general character and lot sizes of the surrounding neighborhood.

Jason Irving and 21 Bridge Street LLC seek a variance to subdivide 21 Bridge Street (R10 district) into two lots, retaining the existing single-family home and creating a new lot for the construction of an additional single-family home. The applicant characterizes the proposal as consistent with the neighborhood's general character and surrounding lot sizes. This is a continued public hearing with no new continuation date stated.

Continued variance hearing to subdivide the existing lot at 21 Bridge Street, retain the existing single-family home, and create a new second lot for construction of an additional single-family home consistent with neighborhood character in the R10 district.

stories.name: Boston Consignment Retail and Storage Use Variance

contacts.name: Joseph P. Coughlin, Trustee of Gateway II Trust of 1997

events.summary: Variance requested to allow approximately 9,000 sq.ft. of an existing 51,158 sq.ft. building for retail use by Boston Consignment (70% of business conducted online) and 12,000 sq.ft. for associated storage and office use in the IR zoning district.

stories.name: 12 Tozer Road Wireless Communications Facility

contacts.name: Cellco Partnership d/b/a Verizon Wireless and the Towers, LLC

events.summary: Continued hearing for a Special Permit under §300-42(C)(1)(p) and height and setback variances under §300-25A to install a wireless communications facility with a 124-foot monopole (including 4-foot lightning rod) at approximately 52-foot setback from the property line in the IR district, Map 68, Parcel 25B.

stories.name: Accessory Dwelling Unit Ordinance Update – Order #119

events.summary: The Planning Board set a Joint Public Hearing with the Beverly City Council for May 4, 2026 at 7:30 p.m. to consider Order #119, an update to the city's Accessory Dwelling Unit Ordinance. No substantive details of the proposed changes were discussed.

stories.name: 85-87 Bridge Street Definitive Subdivision Plan

contacts.name: Robert Noyes II & Stephanie Marie Noyes

events.summary: The Planning Board endorsed the mylars for the previously approved Definitive Subdivision Plan at 85-87 Bridge Street, a frontage-waiver case for owners Robert Noyes II & Stephanie Marie Noyes. The appeal period has lapsed and the City Clerk confirmed its status; no covenant was required.

stories.name: 39 and 41 Woodbury Street ANR Lot Merger

contacts.name: Wrecking Crew LLC c/o Joe Cunningham

events.summary: The Planning Board endorsed an Approval Not Required (ANR) plan to remove the lot line between 39 and 41 Woodbury Street, merging them into a single parcel. The combined lot meets frontage and area requirements for the zoning district; no new construction scope was described.

stories.name: 6 Greenleaf Drive, Beverly, MA

brief.what: David Veritas is seeking a variance at 6 Greenleaf Drive (R15 district) in Beverly, MA to construct a detached accessory structure combining a 2-car garage and an accessory dwelling unit (ADU), to be sited in front of the existing single-family home. The proposed structure meets all setback and height requirements; the variance from Section 300-27B(2) is required solely because of its placement in front of the principal dwelling.
brief.status: A public hearing before the Beverly Zoning Board of Appeals was held on March 25, 2026.
brief.whats_next: No next step stated in the record.
brief.outlook: The project's limited variance scope — needed only for siting placement, not for setbacks or height — suggests a relatively straightforward case before the board, which may favor a favorable outcome, though no board decision is yet on record.

events.summary: New public hearing on a variance application to construct a detached accessory structure combining a 2-car garage and an accessory dwelling unit (ADU), to be sited in front of the existing single-family home in the R15 district. The proposed structure meets all setbacks and height requirements; variance from Section 300-27B(2) is required solely for the siting in front of the principal dwelling.

David Veritas seeks a variance at 6 Greenleaf Drive (R15 district) to construct a new detached accessory structure combining a 2-car garage and an accessory dwelling unit (ADU) in front of the existing single-family home. The proposed structure is stated to meet all setback and height requirements.

stories.name: 68 Paine Avenue New Single-Family House and Cabana Conversion

events.summary: Listed as an extension request on the agenda, this application seeks a Finding and Variances to construct a new single-family house with a pile-supported elevated driveway on a beachfront lot in the R90 district, while partially demolishing the existing home and converting it to an accessory beach cabana. Relief requested includes side setback variances for the main residence, driveway court, and driveway ramp, plus a height variance for the converted cabana at its existing approximately 20-foot height.

stories.name: Gillis Park Reconstruction

events.summary: The Conservation Commission closed the hearing and issued an Order of Conditions under DEP File #5-1463 for the City of Beverly to reconstruct Gillis Park: converting a full basketball court to a half court with a pavilion, and adding a patio paver entrance with seating wall, flagpole, and ADA-compliant ramp outside the buffer zones. The project reduces overall impervious area within Coastal Bank and Riverfront Area resource zones and includes planting of three white flowering dogwood trees.

stories.name: Boyles Street Sidewalk Project

events.summary: City of Beverly brings a potential sidewalk construction project at 90 and 92 Boyles Street to the Conservation Commission for discussion, noting the project abuts a vernal pool. No formal filing has been made; the item is listed under Other Business as an informational discussion.

stories.name: Lothrop Street Memorial ADA Walkway — Minor Modification

events.summary: The Conservation Commission approved a minor modification under DEP File #5-1455 to add an ADA-compliant asphalt walkway spur to the existing Lothrop Street memorial, involving approximately 368 sq ft of disturbance within the 100-foot buffer zone. The improvement was funded through the Community Preservation Committee and carries a hard completion deadline of end of June 2026 as a condition of the funding.

stories.name: 269 Dodge Street Residential Addition, Pool, and Site Reconstruction

events.summary: The Conservation Commission closed the hearing and issued an Order of Conditions under DEP File #5-1461 for a multi-element residential project near a pond at 269 Dodge Street: demolition of an existing driveway, greenhouse, and patio; construction of a new addition; replacement of a deck; and installation of a therapeutic pool, patio, and retaining wall. Special conditions govern soil stockpiling outside buffer zones, tree removal by hand tools, chlorinated pool drainage away from wetlands, and use of ACQ pressure-treated lumber for the deck.

events.project_name: Beverly High School Cafeteria Redesign and Renovation (Bid #26-015)
events.summary: The City of Beverly is seeking sealed bids for the redesign, renovation, and revitalization of the cafeteria space at Beverly High School (Bid #26-015). Sealed bids are due June 25, 2026 at 11:00 AM. Bid documents are available at https://www.beverlyma.gov/Bids.aspx or from the Office of the Purchasing Agent, Room 24, 191 Cabot St., Beverly City Hall during regular business hours.
